items.title: the reason Dav Pilkey spells his name that way rules
items.author: Seth Rosenthal
items.body_excerpt:
the reason Dav Pilkey spells his name that way rules

www.theguardian.com/childrens-bo...

images.alt:
How do you pronounce your name?
It's Dave and the reason, I think, people get mixed up is because it's spelt "Dav", which is untraditional. I used to work at Pizza Hut - do you have Pizza Huts here in the UK? Well, this was more than 30 years ago and I was a waiter at Pizza Hut. They made a name tag for me and they had a little label-maker so when they were making my name and they punched in D
- chickuk - and A - chickuk - and V - chuckuk
- but when they got to E - urggh - it didn't work, so it's just Dav. I don't know why that's stuck but everyone's started calling me Dav and spelling my name like that but, yeah, it's been Dav ever since.

items.title: Hollinger POTD Sebastian Telfair. Brooklyn HS PG was drafted 13th by POR in 04. Was super quick but had trouble w/jumper. After 2 yrs, traded to BOS. Spent a yr in BOS but traded to MIN in KG trade & then bounced around thru 14-15. Also played in China. Has had legal issues.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst

items.title: Victor Wembanyama's 70 blocks this Playoffs are the MOST by a player in his postseason debut (since blocks became official in 1974), surpassing Dikembe Mutombo (69) in 1994!
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
Victor Wembanyama's 70 blocks this Playoffs are the MOST by a player in his postseason debut (since blocks became official in 1974), surpassing Dikembe Mutombo (69) in 1994!

The Spurs' young star is back in action tomorrow night in Game 4 of the NBA Finals at 8:30pm/et on ABC 🏆

items.title: Sergio De Larrea has an impressive feel for the game; he shot over 40% from beyond the arc, & he's a playmaker when orchestrating the offense.
items.author: Bobby Krivitsky
items.body_excerpt:
Sergio De Larrea has an impressive feel for the game; he shot over 40% from beyond the arc, & he's a playmaker when orchestrating the offense.

He also brings another offensive trait the Celtics would love. And that's to say nothing of the disruptive abilities of a defender w/ a 6'9" wingspan.

1/2

items.title: I'm not sure why there's so much kvetching over the Knick offense in Game 3 when they scored at worst in the league levels in the first 2 games and had a 119 ortg last night. They lost because their defense wasn't nearly to the level of the first 2 games.
items.author: Nate Duncan
items.body_excerpt: I'm not sure why there's so much kvetching over the Knick offense in Game 3 when they scored at worst in the league levels in the first 2 games and had a 119 ortg last night. They lost because their defense wasn't nearly to the level of the first 2 games.
